questions.statement: <p>Which Fundamental Duty was added by the 86<sup>th</sup>&nbsp;Amendment Act?</p>
options: To provide opportunities for education to his/her child or ward between the age of six and fourteen years. /
To value and preserve the rich heritage of the country&rsquo;s composite culture. /
To uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India. /
To safeguard public property and to abjure violence. /

questions.statement: Protection of _______&nbsp;is the fundamental duty of an Indian citizen.&nbsp;
options: holy scriptures&nbsp; /
children and old people&nbsp;&nbsp; /
villages&nbsp; /
wildlife&nbsp; /

questions.statement: Which of the following is the Fundamental Duty of the citizen of India?
options: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion /
To express one&#39;s opinions, views, and beliefs freely /
To move freely throughout the territory of the country /
To preserve&nbsp;the culture of the nation. /

questions.statement: Which of the following is <em><strong>NOT</strong></em> a fundamental duty?
options: To protect monuments and places of national importance /
To protect and improve the natural environment /
To safeguard public property /
To respect the National Anthem /

questions.statement: Fundamental duties in the Indian constitution are borrowed from the constitution of _______.
options: France /
Canada /
USSR /
Britain /
